topics.title: Foundations of Sanatan Dharma
topics.summary: A beginner-safe orientation to key principles, source layers, and study methods.
keyTakeaways: Begin with concepts before interpretation debates.
Anchor claims in primary and commentarial sources.
Progress from orientation to depth in clear steps.

topics.title: Dharma and Ethical Frameworks
topics.summary: How dharma is understood across duty, context, role, and universal ethics.

topics.title: Karma and Action
topics.summary: A practical overview of action, intention, and consequences in classical sources.

topics.title: Atman and Brahman
topics.summary: A clear introduction to identity, ultimate reality, and major interpretive differences.

topics.title: Upanishadic Inquiry Pathways
topics.summary: How to approach philosophical depth in the Upanishads without losing orientation.

topics.title: Yoga Pathways
topics.summary: A comparative map of karma, jnana, and bhakti-oriented pathways across texts.

texts.title: Bhagavad Gita
texts.overview: The canonical entry point for studying duty, action, devotion, and knowledge in a single dialogue framework.

texts.overview: A guided way into the philosophical core of Vedika through layered reading paths and concept anchors.

guides.excerpt: A calm entry guide for readers who want philosophical depth without losing orientation.

guides.title: Comparing Upanishadic Voices

comparisons.summary: A source-grounded comparison of role-specific duty and universal ethical obligations.

comparisons.summary: A comparative reading map of core identity and reality language across selected Upanishadic passages.
